MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Oct-15-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: THOMAS BORBELY, ESQ., BOORNAZIAN JENSEN & GARTHE, 510.834.4350, FOR DEFENDANT/CROSS DEFENDANT PAUL RYAN ASSOCIATES.
JEAN TANG, ESQ., MURCHISON & CUMMING, 415.524.4300, FOR CROSS DEFENDANT STEPHEN KING CONSTRUCTION, INC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: CROSS DEFENDANT STEPHEN KING CONSTRUCTION, INC.'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND CROSS COMPLAINT AND DISMISS ACTION is granted. Cross-Complainant fails to establish that cross-defendant has minimum contacts with California to warrant personal jurisdiction or that cross-defendant consented to California jurisdiction through the subcontract, which incorporated the prime contract. There is nothing in the prime contract to indicate that the forum selection clause applies to subcontractors. The request for additional discovery on jurisdictional matters is denied because there is no showing of a reasonable possibility that relevant facts in support of jurisdiction are available. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077.

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Oct-21-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Anthony F. Manzo, Esq., from Boornazian, Jensen & Garthe, 510-834-4350, and Kimberly A. Millington, Esq., from Koletsky, Mancini, Feldman & Morrow, 213-427-2350, for Defendant and Cross-Defendant and opposing party Ryan Associates
Harry C. Gilbert, Esq., from Joseph Costella & Associates, 925-945-40004, for Cross-defendant and moving party Dorvin D. Leis co., Inc.

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling:
CROSS DEFENDANT DORVIN D. LEIS CO., INC.'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS is Granted. Cross-complainant Ryan Associates has not met its burden of establishing either sufficient contacts of Dorvin D. Leis Co. with California or a forum selection clause binding that company. The purchase of construction products by a Hawaii company from a California company for use in a Hawaiian construction project for plaintiff is insufficient to establish specific jurisdiction. Nor is the purchase of construction products by a Hawaiian company from California companies for use in Hawaiian projects on an ongoing basis sufficient to establish general jurisdiction. The forum selection clause in the owner/general contractor contract does not become applicable to any disputes with Dorvin D. Leis Co. merely by the language that 'Subcontractor ' will abide by the' General Contract. There is nothing in the language of the forum selection clause in the General Contract that makes it applicable to a subcontractor. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

Off Calendar Sep-19-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
CROSS-DEFENDANT MAUI WATERPROOFING'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OF CROSS-COMPLAINANT PAUL RYAN ASSOCIATES.

APPEARANCES: Anthony F. Manzo, Esq. of Boornazian, Jensen & Garthe 510/834-4350 & Kimberly A. Millington, Esq. of Koletsky, Mancini, Feldman & Morrow 510/272-4460 for deft/x-deft & opposing party Paul Ryan Associates; Kevin P. McCarthy, Esq. of McCarthy & McCarthy, LLP 510/839-8100 for x-deft Maui Waterproofing; George K. Milionis, Esq. of Gordon & Rees LLP 986-5900 for x-deft First Glass, Inc.

RULING: Discussed and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - DENIED. CROSS-DEFENDANT SIGNED A SUBCONTRACT THAT INCORPORATED THE GENERAL CONTRACT BETWEEN CROSS-COMPLAINANT AND PLAINTIFF. SUBCONTRACTOR AGREED THAT IT WAS FAMILIAR WITH THE GENERAL CONTRACT AND ALL ITS TERMS AND AGREED THAT THEY ARE INCLUDED IN THE SUBCONTRACT. THE GENERAL CONTRACT INCLUDED A FORUM SELECTION CLAUSE SPECIFICALLY IDENTIFYING SAN FRANCISCO, CA AS THE PROPER FORUM FOR ALL DISPUTES RESULTING IN ARBITRATION OR LITIGATION. THIS FORUM SELECTION CLAUSE IS VALID AND ENFORCEABLE. In addition, the Court stated that the ruling is without prejudice to bringing a motion for change of venue should discovery bear out such a motion may be warranted. Order signed in open court. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998
